items.title: Appearance Pointers -- Multimodal Region Control of Diffusion Transformers

items.title: CodeRescue: Budget-Calibrated Recovery Routing for Coding Agents
items.summary: Coding agents increasingly operate in executable environments where a failed attempt produces actionable feedback rather than merely an incorrect answer. items.title: Provable diffusion-based posterior sampling for linear inverse problems via DDIM
items.summary: Diffusion-based methods have achieved remarkable empirical success in solving inverse problems. However, many existing posterior samplers either lack rigorous theoretical guarantees or incur substantial computational overhead. items.title: ISO: An RLVR-Native Optimization Stack

items.title: Associative Emotional Learning in Convolutional Neural Networks
items.summary: Associative emotional learning enables organisms to adaptively link pleasant or unpleasant outcomes to the presence of predictive stimuli. items.title: ResearchArena: Evaluating Sabotage and Monitoring in Automated AI R&D
items.summary: As AI agents begin to automate AI R&D, we need ways to assess whether their outputs are safe to deploy, even when the agents themselves may be untrusted. items.title: Off-Context GRPO: Learning to Reason on Hard Problems using Privileged Information
items.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) improves reasoning in large language models. items.title: From Distances to Trajectories: Real-Time Signed Distance Function Mapping and Distance-Accelerated Motion Planning for UAVs
items.summary: Autonomous flight in cluttered environments requires a robot to build a geometric map of its surroundings and plan safe, dynamically feasible trajectories, all onboard and in real time. items.title: Riemannian Deep Learning:Modules, Networks, and Geometries
items.summary: Deep neural networks on manifold-valued representations have attracted growing interest, but many basic components remain tied to specific manifolds, rely on Euclidean approximations, or require costly and numerically fragile geometric operations. items.title: LLM Detection as an Intervention: Downstream Impact under Strategic User Behavior
items.summary: As LLM adoption becomes more widespread, there is a growing interest in detecting LLM-generated content, for example through LLM detection tools and through heuristics based on language patterns. items.title: Graph-Based Agentic AI with LangGraph: Workflow Pathways for Long-Running Stateful Business Processes
items.summary: This paper is a practitioner guide to graph-based workflow pathways for long-running, stateful, multi-step generative AI systems in business processes. items.title: The safety failures we are not instrumenting: a perspective on hidden safety-critical challenges in modern AI systems
items.summary: Current AI safety discourse still focuses disproportionately on visible failures, including obvious harms, dramatic misuse, and hypothetical catastrophic scenarios. items.title: They'll Verify. They Just Won't Act. How Authority Framing and Laundered Code Turn a Trusted Agentic CI/CD Pipeline Into an Attack Surface
items.summary: We study a five-agent CI/CD pipeline (triage -> developer -> security-scan -> review -> approve/deploy), built from five distinct production LLMs across three providers, behind an LLM firewall in shadow mode. items.title: Toward Auditable Fraud Detection: Combining Graph Features, Model Explanations, and Agentic Case Investigation
items.summary: Fraud detection systems must scale with rising transaction volume while remaining explainable and reviewable. items.title: BioSecBench-Surveillance: A Verifiable Benchmark for AI Agents in Pathogen Genomic Surveillance
items.summary: As pathogen genomic surveillance scales, the bottleneck is shifting from data generation to analysis. items.title: PathAgentBench: Benchmarking Evidence-Seeking Vision-Language Models on Whole-Slide Pathology Image

items.title: Benchmarking Generalization in Financial Statement Fraud Detection: robust evaluation and novel tasks

items.title: 1-Lipschitz Neural Networks on Hadamard Manifolds
items.summary: Controlling the Lipschitz constant of a neural network is a standard way to promote robustness and stability. Most existing constraining strategies are designed for Euclidean spaces. items.title: Fundamental limits of distributed multiclass classification from simple binary decisions

items.title: CircuitKIT : Circuit Discovery, Evaluation, and Application Toolkit for Mechanistic Interpretability

items.title: Staypoint Detection from Noisy Trajectory Data [Experiment Paper]
